 {"id":"bd-12u4u","title":"Feature: Ensemble Orchestrator - Spawning and mode assignment","description":"# Feature: Ensemble Orchestrator - 3-Stage Pipeline Coordinator

Orchestrates the full ensemble pipeline: Intake → Mode Run → Synthesis.

## Pipeline Stages

### Stage 1: Intake (Coordinated)
1. Check context pack cache
2. If miss: generate context pack via ContextPackGenerator
3. If context thin: prompt user with targeted questions
4. Cache context pack for reuse

### Stage 2: Mode Run (Parallel)
1. Load ensemble definition (e.g., 'project-diagnosis')
2. Resolve mode IDs to full ReasoningMode objects
3. Generate prompts via PromptInjector (base + preamble + schema)
4. Spawn N agents in parallel tmux windows
5. Monitor progress, token spend, early stop conditions
6. Collect outputs as agents complete

### Stage 3: Synthesis (Sequential)
1. Validate all outputs against OutputSchema
2. Run mechanical merging (grouping, de-duplication)
3. Spawn Synthesizer agent with merged inputs
4. Spawn Disagreement Auditor with synthesis + raw outputs
5. Combine into final report

## EnsembleManager Core
```go
type EnsembleManager struct {
    Config          *EnsembleConfig
    ContextGen      *ContextPackGenerator
    PromptInjector  *PromptInjector
    BudgetTracker   *BudgetTracker
    OutputCollector *OutputCollector
    Synthesizer     *Synthesizer
    Auditor         *DisagreementAuditor
    Metrics         *MetricsCollector
}

func (m *EnsembleManager) Run(ensemble string, problem string) (*EnsembleResult, error)
func (m *EnsembleManager) RunStage1() (*ContextPack, error)
func (m *EnsembleManager) RunStage2(ctx *ContextPack) ([]ModeOutput, error)
func (m *EnsembleManager) RunStage3(outputs []ModeOutput) (*SynthesisReport, error)
```

## Agent Assignment Strategy

### Default: Round-Robin
Distribute modes across available agent types (claude, codex, gemini).

### Affinity-Based
Some modes work better with certain agents:
- Formal modes → Claude (strong logical reasoning)
- Creative modes → Gemini (divergent thinking)
- Analytical modes → Codex (code understanding)

### User Override
Allow explicit agent assignment per mode.

## State Tracking

Track ensemble state for recovery and monitoring:
```go
type EnsembleState struct {
    SessionName  string
    Ensemble     string
    Stage        PipelineStage  // intake, mode_run, synthesis
    Agents       []AgentState
    Budget       BudgetState
    StartedAt    time.Time
}
```

## Files
- internal/ensemble/manager.go - Core orchestration
- internal/ensemble/assignment.go - Agent assignment
- internal/ensemble/state.go - State tracking
- internal/swarm/ensemble_integration.go - Tmux/swarm integration","notes":"Feature complete: EnsembleManager, 3-stage pipeline, assignment strategies, state tracking all implemented. Fixed import cycle and type mismatches to enable ensemble_experimental build. All tests pass.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:25:50.551686121Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T07:46:10.370388893Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T07:46:10.369986895Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-12u4u","depends_on_id":"bd-1kthb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-12u4u","depends_on_id":"bd-2ouwm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-12u4u","depends_on_id":"bd-49fb9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-12u4u","depends_on_id":"bd-n9bcu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-12u4u","depends_on_id":"bd-wx2qv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-12ua0","title":"Task: Write unit tests for Intake & Context Pack system","description":"# Task: Intake System Unit Tests

Comprehensive tests for context generation, questions, and caching.

## Test Coverage

### ContextPackGenerator (context_generator_test.go)
```go
func TestContextPackGenerator_Generate_FullProject(t *testing.T)
func TestContextPackGenerator_Generate_MinimalProject(t *testing.T)
func TestContextPackGenerator_DetectLanguages_Go(t *testing.T)
func TestContextPackGenerator_DetectLanguages_TypeScript(t *testing.T)
func TestContextPackGenerator_DetectLanguages_Mixed(t *testing.T)
func TestContextPackGenerator_DetectFrameworks_GoMod(t *testing.T)
func TestContextPackGenerator_DetectFrameworks_PackageJson(t *testing.T)
func TestContextPackGenerator_ParseReadme_Full(t *testing.T)
func TestContextPackGenerator_ParseReadme_Missing(t *testing.T)
func TestContextPackGenerator_GetRecentCommits(t *testing.T)
func TestContextPackGenerator_CountOpenIssues(t *testing.T)
```

### Targeted Questions (questions_test.go)
```go
func TestShouldAskQuestions_ThinContext(t *testing.T)
func TestShouldAskQuestions_RichContext(t *testing.T)
func TestSelectQuestions_PrioritizesRequired(t *testing.T)
func TestMergeAnswers(t *testing.T)
func TestDefaultQuestions_AllHaveIDs(t *testing.T)
```

### Context Cache (context_cache_test.go)
```go
func TestContextCache_GetPut_RoundTrip(t *testing.T)
func TestContextCache_Get_CacheMiss(t *testing.T)
func TestContextCache_Get_Expired(t *testing.T)
func TestContextCache_Invalidate(t *testing.T)
func TestContextCache_ComputeHash_Deterministic(t *testing.T)
func TestContextCache_IsStale_TTL(t *testing.T)
func TestContextCache_IsStale_GitCommit(t *testing.T)
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log cache hit/miss decisions (project hash, ttl)
- Log detected languages/frameworks for each fixture
- Log thin-context reasons that trigger questions
- Log number of questions selected and merged answers

## Test Fixtures
- testdata/context/full-project/ (go.mod, README, src/)
- testdata/context/minimal-project/ (just main.go)
- testdata/context/no-readme/

- internal/ensemble/questions_test.go
- internal/ensemble/context_cache_test.go","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T08:45:10.080962341Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T02:40:27.304250485Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T02:40:27.303974766Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-12ua0","depends_on_id":"bd-15yvf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-12ua0","depends_on_id":"bd-1azv5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-12ua0","depends_on_id":"bd-1ilu8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-12ua0","depends_on_id":"bd-2ouwm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1dfip","title":"Implement verification logic for restart phases","description":"Implement verification logic for each restart phase.

## Verification Points

### 1. Exit Verification
Check pane output for shell prompt return.
Look for: bash/zsh prompt, $ or >, username@hostname pattern.

### 2. Launch Verification  
Check for claude code ready indicators.
Look for: banner text, prompt character, ready state.

### 3. Prompt Delivery Verification
Verify prompt was received.
Look for: echo of sent text in pane output.

## Implementation
Each verification should:
- Poll with reasonable timeout
- Return detailed status (not just bool)
- Capture relevant output for debugging

## --no-verify Flag
Skip verification for faster operation (less safe).
Use when operator knows environment is stable.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:25:37.143997287Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T02:32:53.699602668Z","closed_at":"2026-01-22T02:32:53.699451924Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dfip","depends_on_id":"bd-21tqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dmet","title":"Task: Implement output capture for ensemble agents in internal/ensemble/capture.go","description":"# Task: Implement Output Capture for Ensemble Agents

## Location
internal/ensemble/capture.go

## Purpose
Capture pane output from each agent in the ensemble and extract the **YAML output schema** for synthesis. Must handle large outputs and malformed YAML gracefully.

## Implementation
```go
package ensemble

type CapturedOutput struct {
    ModeID        string
    PaneName      string
    RawOutput     string
    Parsed        *ModeOutput
    ParseErrors   []error
    CapturedAt    time.Time
    LineCount     int
    TokenEstimate int
}

type OutputCapture struct {
    tmuxClient *tmux.Client
    maxLines   int  // Default 1000
    validator  *SchemaValidator
}

func (c *OutputCapture) CaptureAll(session *EnsembleSession) ([]CapturedOutput, error)
func (c *OutputCapture) capturePane(pane string) (string, error)
func (c *OutputCapture) extractYAML(raw string) (string, bool)
```

## YAML Extraction Strategy
- Prefer first fenced ```yaml block
- Fallback: detect `thesis:` root line and capture until EOF
- If multiple YAML blocks, choose the largest valid parse

## Integration with Synthesis
- Parsed ModeOutput is passed to OutputCollector
- RawOutput retained for debugging
- Token estimates via internal/tokens package
- **Do not fail if mode_id missing**; OutputCollector will inject from assignment metadata via Normalize

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Captures output from all ensemble panes
- [ ] Extracts YAML schema blocks reliably
- [ ] Handles missing/malformed output gracefully
- [ ] Token estimation for synthesis planning
- [ ] Configurable max lines to capture
- [ ] Logs per-pane capture + parse outcomes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:39:44.994035314Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T15:48:03.170149202Z","closed_at":"2026-01-24T15:48:03.170130136Z","close_reason":"Implemented output capture and extraction logic","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dmet","depends_on_id":"bd-1wnxl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dt84","title":"Feature: Budget & Resource Control - Token caps and early stopping","description":"# Feature: Budget & Resource Control

**Critical for preventing token black holes.** Ensemble runs can be expensive - this feature provides predictability and control.

## Budget Configuration

### BudgetConfig (consistent with types.go)
```yaml
budget:
  per_agent: 4000      # Max tokens per mode agent
  total: 50000         # Max for entire ensemble run
  synthesis: 8000      # Reserved for synthesis phase
  context_pack: 2000   # Reserved for context injection
```

### Early Stopping (Marginal Utility)
Stop when new findings rate drops below threshold:
```go
type EarlyStopConfig struct {
    MinAgentsBeforeStop int     // Run at least N agents
    FindingsThreshold   float64 // Stop if new findings/token < threshold
    SimilarityThreshold float64 // Stop if output similarity > threshold
    WindowSize          int     // Evaluate over last N agents
}
```

### Context Pack Caching
- Cache generated context packs
- Reuse across ensemble runs
- Invalidate on code changes or explicit request
- Track cache hit rate in metrics

## Real-Time Monitoring

### Token Velocity Dashboard
```
Budget: 23,456 / 50,000 tokens (47%)
████████████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░

Agent Spend:
[0] F1 Argument Mapping    3,847 tokens  ✓
[1] E2 Root Cause          4,012 tokens  ✓ (cap hit)
[2] K1 Systems Thinking    2,891 tokens  ◐
[3] L3 Meta-cognitive      pending
[4] H2 Strategic           pending

Findings Rate: 2.3 findings/1k tokens
Early Stop: disabled (4 more agents needed)
```

## Alerts & Guardrails
- Warn at 80% budget consumed
- Auto-stop at 100% with partial synthesis
- Log all cap hits for tuning
- Suggest budget adjustments based on history

## Files
- internal/ensemble/budget.go - Budget types and tracking
- internal/ensemble/early_stop.go - Marginal utility detection
- internal/ensemble/metrics.go - Findings velocity, similarity
- internal/tui/budget_panel.go - Dashboard component","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:57:48.239196966Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T01:50:26.586537664Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T01:50:26.586517856Z","close_reason":"Core Budget & Resource Control implemented: BudgetTracker (budget.go) with per-agent/total tracking and enforcement, EarlyStopDetector (early_stop.go) with findings rate and similarity thresholds, token estimation via internal/tokens, ContextPackCache (context_cache.go) with TTL and invalidation. All components have comprehensive unit tests and integration tests. EarlyStop is wired into pipeline stage2. TUI dashboard (metrics_panel.go) and detailed metrics are scoped to bd-397f0 (Observability Metrics).","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dt84","depends_on_id":"bd-12u4u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1dt84","depends_on_id":"bd-49fb9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
@@ -122,7 +122,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1g5t8","title":"Task: Extend balanced strategy with assignment tracking","description":"## Overview
Balanced strategy already exists. This task extends it to use **real assignment tracking data** (from `bd-92e1`) and improves tie‑breakers without changing the public strategy name.

## Why
Current balanced behavior assumes assignment counts are populated. Without tracking, all counts are zero, so the strategy degenerates to simple capability scoring. We want true load‑aware balancing once tracking is available.

## Changes (extend, don’t replace)
- Wire assignment tracking into `assign.Agent.Assignments` (live counts per agent).
- Add optional tie‑breakers:
  1) fewer active assignments
  2) idle status
  3) least‑recent assignment timestamp
  4) best capability score
- Keep deterministic ordering for identical inputs.

## Inputs
- Assignment tracker state (`bd-92e1`).
- Agent health/availability (if available in current context).

## Outputs
- Deterministic assignment map (pane -> bead).
- Robot output includes per‑agent load snapshot and reasons (if present).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Balanced strategy still returns consistent assignments for the same inputs.
- Assignment counts reflect real state when tracking is enabled.
- Graceful fallback when tracking data is unavailable.
- Tests updated to cover load‑aware tie‑breakers.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:57:40.168441307Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:31:07.660098480Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T18:31:07.660054547Z","close_reason":"Implemented load-aware balanced strategy with tie-breakers: 1) fewer active assignments, 2) capability score, 3) least-recently assigned, 4) lower pane index for determinism. Added tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1g5t8","depends_on_id":"bd-92e1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1g5t8","depends_on_id":"bd-k3zq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1g9wv","title":"Feature: Robot Status Enrichments","description":"## Overview

Enrich the existing `ntm --robot-status` output with critical operational data that
is currently missing but essential for automated swarm management.

## Background

The current --robot-status returns basic pane information (index, agent_type, is_active)
but lacks the data needed to:
- Detect rate-limited panes
- Identify hung processes
- Know which PID to kill for restart
- Track output freshness for stuck detection

## Proposed Schema Addition

Each pane object in the status output should include:

```json
{
  "index": 2,
  "agent_type": "claude",
  "pid": 12345,                    // Shell PID (already available)
  "child_pid": 12346,              // NEW: The actual agent process PID
  "last_output_ts": "2026-01-19T...", // NEW: Last output timestamp
  "rate_limit_detected": false,    // NEW: Pattern match on recent output
  "process_state": "running",      // NEW: running|sleeping|zombie|dead
  "memory_mb": 450,                // NEW: Process memory usage
  "output_lines_since_last": 42    // NEW: Activity indicator
}
```

## Justification for Each Field

1. **child_pid**: Required for kill -9 when soft restart fails. Currently operators
   must run `pgrep -P <shell_pid>` manually.

2. **last_output_ts**: Enables timeout-based stuck detection without external state.
   "No output for 5 minutes" = likely stuck.

3. **rate_limit_detected**: Pattern match for "You've hit your limit" or similar.
   This is the #1 failure mode in practice. Must be detected programmatically.

4. **process_state**: From /proc/<pid>/status. Zombie processes need different handling
   than running ones.

5. **memory_mb**: Detect runaway processes consuming excessive memory.

6. **output_lines_since_last**: Quick activity indicator without timestamp parsing.

## Implementation Notes

- child_pid: Use pgrep -P or parse /proc/<pid>/task
- last_output_ts: Track during capture-pane operations
- rate_limit_detected: Grep last N lines of pane output
- process_state: Read /proc/<pid>/status State field
- memory_mb: Read /proc/<pid>/status VmRSS field
- These should be gathered efficiently (single pass where possible)

## Performance Considerations

- Status is called frequently; keep it fast
- Pattern matching for rate limits should be bounded (last 50 lines)
- Process state reads are cheap (procfs)
- Consider caching with short TTL if needed

## Testing Requirements

- Unit tests for each field extraction
- E2E tests with actual stuck/rate-limited agents
- Performance benchmarks for large swarms (20+ panes)","notes":"Robot Status Enrichments feature is complete. All essential fields implemented: rate_limit_detected (bd-1mcvs), child_pid (bd-36dd7), last_output_ts (bd-3hxdl), process_state (bd-3ivl8), memory_mb (bd-lo0ko). The output_lines_since_last field (bd-33tuj) was deliberately skipped for performance reasons per existing comment in status_enrich.go.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:13:19.647496277Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T02:41:21.086289872Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T02:41:21.086191316Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1g9wv","depends_on_id":"bd-258a1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1gbt5","title":"Add ntm assign --reassign for moving bead between agents","description":"## Overview
Allow users to move an assignment from one agent to another without losing tracking state.

## Use Cases
1. Agent is stuck and user wants to give bead to a faster agent
2. User realizes a bead is better suited for a different agent type
3. Load balancing - redistribute work mid-session

## Usage
```bash
# Reassign bead to a different pane
ntm assign myproject --reassign bd-xyz --to-pane=4

# Reassign to specific agent type (picks idle agent of that type)
ntm assign myproject --reassign bd-xyz --to-type=codex

# Reassign with new prompt
ntm assign myproject --reassign bd-xyz --to-pane=4 --prompt="Continue from where the previous agent left off"
```

## Workflow
1. Validate bead is currently assigned
2. Get current assignment info
3. Update assignment status to "reassigned"
4. Create new assignment for target agent
5. Release file reservations from old agent (if any)
6. Create file reservations for new agent (if any)
7. Send prompt to new agent
8. Return reassignment result

## Output (Standard Envelope)
```json
{
  "command": "assign",
  "subcommand": "reassign",
  "session": "myproject",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-19T12:00:00Z",
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "reassigned": {
      "bead_id": "bd-xyz",
      "bead_title": "Implement feature X",
      "pane": 4,
      "agent_type": "codex",
      "agent_name": "BlueLake",
      "status": "assigned",
      "prompt_sent": true,
      "assigned_at": "2026-01-19T12:00:00Z",
      "previous_pane": 2,
      "previous_agent": "GreenCastle",
      "previous_agent_type": "claude",
      "previous_status": "working",
      "file_reservations_transferred": true
    }
  },
  "warnings": [],
  "error": null
}
```

## Error Output
```json
{
  "command": "assign",
  "subcommand": "reassign",
  "session": "myproject",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-19T12:00:00Z",
  "success": false,
  "data": null,
  "warnings": [],
  "error": {
    "code": "TARGET_BUSY",
    "message": "pane 4 already has assignment bd-abc",
    "details": {
      "current_bead": "bd-abc",
      "current_status": "working"
    }
  }
}
```

## Error Codes
- NOT_ASSIGNED: Bead doesn't have an active assignment
- TARGET_BUSY: Target pane already has an assignment
- PANE_NOT_FOUND: Target pane doesn't exist
- NO_IDLE_AGENT: No idle agent of specified type

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Reassign bead to specific pane
- [ ] Reassign by agent type (auto-select idle)
- [ ] Transfer file reservations via Agent Mail
- [ ] Update assignment store correctly
- [ ] Optional custom prompt
- [ ] Standard JSON envelope output
- [ ] Clear error codes

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Assignment state update logic
- Unit test: File reservation transfer
- Unit test: Output format validation
- E2E test: Full reassignment workflow","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T21:36:52.859683490Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T03:55:18.854201422Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T03:55:18.854147310Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1gbt5","depends_on_id":"bd-1ohy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1gbt5","depends_on_id":"bd-92e1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1gbt5","depends_on_id":"bd-k3zq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1ghcp","title":"RCH: Implement rch binary detection and worker availability check","description":"Create utility function to detect RCH and check worker status.

## Requirements
1. Check if 'rch' binary exists on PATH
2. Verify rch version is compatible
3. Check if any workers are configured and available
4. Cache result with short TTL (workers may come/go)

## Implementation Location
Create new file: internal/integrations/rch/detect.go

```go
package rch

import (
    "encoding/json"
    "os/exec"
    "sync"
    "time"
)

type RCHStatus struct {
    Available    bool
    Version      string
    WorkerCount  int
    HealthyCount int
    LastCheck    time.Time
}

var (
    mu     sync.RWMutex
    status RCHStatus
    ttl    = 30 * time.Second
)

func GetStatus() RCHStatus {
    mu.RLock()
    if time.Since(status.LastCheck) < ttl {
        defer mu.RUnlock()
        return status
    }
    mu.RUnlock()
    
    return refresh()
}

func refresh() RCHStatus {
    mu.Lock()
    defer mu.Unlock()
    
    // Check binary
    // Run 'rch status --json'
    // Parse worker info
    status.LastCheck = time.Now()
    return status
}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:07:32.155904396Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T10:07:34.350046341Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T10:07:34.349997629Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete: internal/tools/rch.go with RCHAdapter following the DCG adapter pattern. Includes binary detection, version checking, worker availability with 30s TTL caching. Tests in internal/tools/rch_test.go all pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ghcp","depends_on_id":"bd-1c9ib","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}

-{"id":"bd-1zrjo","title":"TOON: Implement serialization wrapper supporting JSON and TOON","description":"Create abstraction layer for robot mode output serialization.

## Requirements

1. Single interface for serialization
2. Support JSON and TOON backends
3. Runtime switchable (per-command or global)
4. Handle errors gracefully (fallback to JSON)

## Implementation

```go
package serialize

type Format string

const (
    FormatJSON Format = "json"
    FormatTOON Format = "toon"
)

type Serializer interface {
    Serialize(v any) ([]byte, error)
    ContentType() string
}

func NewSerializer(format Format) Serializer {
    switch format {
    case FormatTOON:
        return &TOONSerializer{}
    default:
        return &JSONSerializer{}
    }
}
```

## Code Location
- internal/serialize/serialize.go
- internal/serialize/json.go
- internal/serialize/toon.go

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Serializer interface defined with Serialize() and ContentType() methods
2. JSONSerializer implements interface using encoding/json
3. TOONSerializer implements interface calling toon binary
4. NewSerializer factory function returns correct implementation
5. Fallback to JSON when toon binary not found
6. Error wrapping provides clear context

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/serialize/serialize_test.go
- Test round-trip JSON encoding/decoding
- Test TOON encoding with mock binary
- Test fallback behavior when toon not available

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
# [E2E-SERIALIZER] Serialization Wrapper Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-SERIALIZER]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing serialization wrapper"
# Test JSON serialization works
TEST_DATA='{"key":"value"}'
echo "$TEST_DATA" | go run ./cmd/serialize-test/ --format json | jq -e '.' || exit 1
echo "$LOG_PREFIX JSON serialization passed"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. Serialization wrapper supports JSON and TOON
2. Same Go types work with both formats
3. TOON output 40-60 percent smaller than JSON
4. Round-trip parsing verified
5. Unit tests for both formats
6. Benchmark comparing sizes","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:03:31.235798416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:13:18.555102773Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1zrjo","depends_on_id":"bd-1u462","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zrjo","depends_on_id":"bd-6wc0a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zrjo","depends_on_id":"bd-77bxi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1zs9y","title":"Feature: Token Budget Advisor","description":"## Summary
Estimate token usage for selected modes and provide budget-aware recommendations before running an ensemble.

## Rationale
Token costs can be significant; users need visibility and alternatives.

## Implementation
- Per-mode estimation using token heuristics (bd-2t4cs) + historical calibration.
- `ntm ensemble estimate` shows per-mode breakdown + total estimate.
- `--budget` flag warns and suggests cheaper alternatives.
- Track actual vs. estimated to improve calibration over time.

## Logging
- Log estimates, calibration deltas, and budget warnings.

## Tests
- Unit: estimation accuracy + calibration adjustment
- Integration: budget warnings with sample configs
- E2E: estimate output + budget warning path

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Reasonably accurate estimates
- [ ] Per-mode breakdown available
- [ ] Budget warnings fire at thresholds
- [ ] Alternative mode suggestions
- [ ] Calibration improves over time

## Dependencies
- Budget control + tracking (bd-1dt84, bd-3d4tl)
- Token estimation heuristics (bd-2t4cs)
- Mode catalog (bd-11mtt)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:09:08.646964343Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:47:34.424225995Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1zs9y","depends_on_id":"bd-11mtt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zs9y","depends_on_id":"bd-1dt84","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zs9y","depends_on_id":"bd-1h4ox","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zs9y","depends_on_id":"bd-2t4cs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zs9y","depends_on_id":"bd-3d4tl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-204ic","title":"Task: REST core endpoints (system, sessions, panes, agents)","description":"## Scope\nImplement REST endpoints for system health/config and session/agent/pane management.\n\n## Endpoints (per Opus plan)\n- System: /health, /version, /deps, /config (GET/PATCH), /doctor.\n- Sessions: list/create/get/kill/spawn/quick/attach/view/zoom/snapshot/watch.\n- Panes: list/get/input/interrupt/output/capture/title.\n- Agents: list/add/send/interrupt/wait/route/activity/health/context/rotate.\n\n## Logging\n- Log session + pane + agent identifiers in every handler.\n\n## Tests\n- Unit: handler validation + error codes.\n- Integration: spawn session, send prompt, verify output tail.\n- E2E (CLI parity): REST results match robot outputs.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- All listed endpoints respond with robot‑style envelopes.\n- Error codes match robot mode (SESSION_NOT_FOUND, PANE_NOT_FOUND, etc.).\n- Parity tests pass.\n","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:49:54.889532345Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T18:49:21.143873939Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T18:49:21.143851577Z","close_reason":"All REST core endpoints implemented: System (/health, /version, /deps, /config, /doctor), Sessions (list/create/get/kill/spawn/quick/attach/view/zoom/snapshot/watch), Panes (list/get/input/interrupt/output/capture/title), Agents (list/spawn/send/interrupt/wait/route/activity/health/context/restart). Build passes, tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-204ic","depends_on_id":"bd-3fqg9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-204ic","depends_on_id":"bd-yn7qe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:03Z","created_by":"import"}]}
